activity_1_b.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වචන නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

activity_1_d.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වාක්‍ය නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

activity_1_e.text:
Read along.
පිටපත සමඟ කියවන්න.
ஒரே நேரத்தில் வாசியுங்கள்.

content.sentence_text: This 't'restle is better than the old one under heavy formwork.
The 't'oggle clamp is tighter than standard clamps during steel fabrication.
The 't'amping rammer is louder than plate compactors in confined spaces.
The new 't'rack roller is smoother than the worn roller on excavators.

example.text:
Listen to the model dialogues.
ආදර්ශ සංවාදවලට සවන් දෙන්න.
மாதிரி உரையாடல்களை கேளுங்கள்.

activity_2_a.text:
Match the words with the pictures.
පින්තූර සමඟ වචන ගලපන්න.
சொற்களைப் படங்களுடன் பொருத்துங்கள்.

activity_2_c.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වාක්‍ය නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

activity_2_d.text:
Read along.
පිටපත සමඟ කියවන්න.
ஒரே நேரத்தில் வாசியுங்கள்.

activity_2_e.text:
Listen to each question, use the pause to answer it, then compare your response with the model answer.
සෑම ප්‍රශ්නයකටම සවන් දෙන්න, පිළිතුරු දීමට විරාමය භාවිතා කරන්න, ඉන්පසු ඔබේ පිළිතුර ආදර්ශ පිළිතුර සමඟ සසඳන්න.
ஒவ்வொரு கேள்வியையும் கவனமாகக் கேட்டு, இடைநிறுத்தத்தின் போது பதிலளிக்கவும், அதன் பிறகு உங்கள் பதிலை மாதிரிப் பதிலுடன் ஒப்பிட்டுப் பார்க்கவும்.

example_dialogues.text_a: Shall I buy these almonds or those almonds?
example_dialogues.text_c: These ones are drier and bigger.

example_dialogues.text_a: Shall I buy this mattress or that mattress?
example_dialogues.text_c: That one is firmer and heavier.

example_dialogues.text_a: Shall I buy this speaker or that speaker?
example_dialogues.text_c: This one is louder and clearer.

dialogues.text_a: Shall I buy this paint brush or that paint brush?
dialogues.text_c: This one is stiffer and lighter.

dialogues.text_a: Shall I buy this piece of wood or that piece of wood?
dialogues.text_c: This one is smoother and harder.

dialogues.text_a: Shall I buy this frying pan or that frying pan?
dialogues.text_c: That one is deeper and flatter.

dialogues.text_a: Shall I buy these metal bars or those metal bars?
dialogues.text_c: Those bars are tougher and stronger.

dialogues.text_a: Shall I buy these dress shoes or those dress shoes?
dialogues.text_c: These ones are flatter and lighter.

dialogues.text_a: Shall I buy this tea or that tea?
dialogues.text_c: This one is lighter and cheaper.

activity_a.text:
Fill in the blanks.
 හිස් තැන් පුරවන්න.
 வெற்றிடங்களை நிரப்புக.

activity_b.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වාක්‍ය නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

content.question: A thick formwork panel is __________ than a thin panel during concrete casting. (stiff)
A reinforced concrete column is __________ than a plain concrete column under load. (hard)
A freshly levelled floor surface becomes __________ after final finishing. (flat)
A heavy-duty wheelbarrow is __________ than a light one on uneven ground. (heavy)
New construction timber is __________ than timber exposed to weather for years. (strong)
A properly tightened anchor bolt feels __________ than a loose one. (firm)
A well-supported scaffold structure is __________ during use than one with poor bracing. (tough)
A dry scaffold platform is __________ than a wet one during work at height. (safe)
A wide access ramp is __________ than a narrow ramp for moving materials. (wide)

activity_b.text: A thick formwork panel is stiffer than a thin panel during concrete casting.
A reinforced concrete column is harder than a plain concrete column under load.
A freshly levelled floor surface becomes flatter after final finishing.
A heavy-duty wheelbarrow is heavier than a light one on uneven ground.
New construction timber is stronger than timber exposed to weather for years.
A properly tightened anchor bolt feels firmer than a loose one.
A well-supported scaffold structure is tougher during use than one with poor bracing.
A dry scaffold platform is safer than a wet one during work at height.
A wide access ramp is wider than a narrow ramp for moving materials.

activity_a.text:
Listen to the audio which will be played twice and click on the correct answer. It is very important that you read the questions before you listen to the recording.
ඔබට සෑම පටිගත කිරීමක්ම දෙවරක් ඇසෙනු ඇත. නිවැරදි පිළිතුර ක්ලික් කරන්න. රෙකෝඩින් එක අහන්නට පෙර ප්‍රශ්නය කියවීම ඉතාමත් වැදගත්ය.
ஒவ்வொரு ஒலிப்பதிவையும் நீங்கள் இருமுறை கேட்கலாம். கேட்ட எண்களை குறித்து எழுதுங்கள். பதிவைக் கேட்பதற்கு முன் கேள்விகளைப் படிப்பது மிகவும் முக்கியம்.

activity_b.text:
Listen.
கேளுங்கள்.
සවන් දෙන්න.

activity_c.text:
Listen and repeat.
සවන් දී වාක්‍ය නැවත කියන්න.
கேட்டு மீண்டும் சொல்லுங்கள்.

activity_d.text: Read along. පිටපත සමඟ කියවන්න. ஒரே நேரத்தில் வாசியுங்கள்.

Questions.question: Why does the instructor say the floor must be cleaned before work begins?
Questions.answer: Because a clean floor is safer than a wet or dirty floor.
options: Because a clean floor looks better than a dirty one.
Because a clean floor is safer than a wet or dirty floor.
Because a dry floor looks better than a dirty one.

Questions.question: What health problems can dirty work surfaces cause, according to the instructor?
Questions.answer: Germs grow faster on dirty surfaces than on clean ones.
options: Germs grow faster on clean surfaces in warm conditions.
Germs grow slower on smooth surfaces than rough ones.
Germs grow faster on dirty surfaces than on clean ones.

Questions.question: Why are dry tool handles important during practical work?
Questions.answer: A drier handle is firmer than a wet one, giving better control.
options: A drier handle is firmer than a wet one, giving better control.
A wet handle is softer than a dry one and easier to hold, giving better control.
A loose handle is easier to hold than a tight one, giving better control.

Questions.question: What does the instructor say about lighting in the work area?
Questions.answer: A brighter area is better lit than a dark one, making hazards easier to see.
options: A dark area is quieter than a bright one, making hazards easier to see.
A brighter area is better lit than a dark one, making hazards easier to see.
Poor lighting is more comfortable than bright lighting for the eyes.

Questions.question: Why should waste be removed regularly from the work area?
Questions.answer: Old waste becomes more harmful than fresh waste.
options: Old waste becomes more harmful than fresh waste.
Fresh waste is heavier than old waste and more harmful than old waste.
A lighter load is more harmful than a heavy one to carry.

activity_b.text: That’s the end of our session for today!
But before we leave the workshop, we are going to clean and organise our work area so that it will be spotless when we come tomorrow morning.
This is not only about looking neat.
A clean area is healthier than a dirty one, and it helps you work more safely throughout the day.
When the work area is organised, you can also move more easily and avoid unnecessary stress.
Sir, do we really need to clean everything before leaving?
Yes, we do.
A cleaner floor is safer than a wet or dirty floor.
Dust, oil, and small spills can make the surface more slippery, which increases the risk of slipping and falling.
Dry areas are very important for your health especially when many people are working in the same space.
What about the tables and tools?
Do we need to clean them as well?
Definitely!
Work surfaces should be wiped properly before use.
A cleaner table is more hygienic than one covered with oil or dust.
Germs grow faster on dirty surfaces, especially in warm conditions.
Clean tools are also safer than dirty ones because they are easier to handle and less likely to slip from your hands.
Sometimes the tools feel hard to grip.
That often happens when handles are greasy or wet.
A drier handle is firmer than a wet one, and a tighter grip gives you better control.
Loose items can fall more easily, and falling tools are more dangerous than tools kept in the correct place.
Does lighting matter during cleaning?
Very much.
A brighter area is better lit than a dark one, so you can see dirt, spills, and hazards more clearly.
Poor lighting makes work more difficult and can strain your eyes over time.
What about rubbish and waste?
Waste must be removed regularly.
A lighter load of waste is easier to carry than a heavy one.
Old waste can become more harmful than fresh waste because it attracts pests and produces bad smells.
So remember, a cleaner, drier, and brighter work area is healthier than a messy one.
Cleaning at the end of the day makes starting work in the morning safer, easier, and more productive.

instrction_a.text:
Watch this video to help you better understand today’s lesson.
අද පාඩම හොඳින් අවබෝධ කර ගැනීමට මෙම වීඩියෝව බලන්න.
இன்றைய பாடத்தை நன்றாக புரிந்துகொள்ள இந்த வீடியோவைப் பாருங்கள்.

instruction_b.text:
These steps will help you to learn English from this video.
මෙම වීඩියෝවෙන් ඉංග්‍රීසි ඉගෙන ගැනීමට මෙම පියවර ඔබට උපකාරී වනු ඇත.
இந்த வீடியோவிலிருந்து ஆங்கிலம் கற்க இந்த படிகள் உங்களுக்கு உதவும்.
steps:
Watch the video 2 times.
මේ වීඩියෝ එක දෙවරක් බලන්න.
வீடியோவை இரண்டு முறைப் பாருங்கள்.

Learn new words using Google Translate.
Google පරිවර්තකය භාවිතයෙන් නව වචන ඉගෙන ගන්න.
கூகுள் ட்ரான்ஸ்லேட் பயன்படுத்தி புதிய சொற்களைக் கற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.
